---
title: "Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives"
---

# Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives

Range: `1421-1512`.

| 1440  | [consistent](#rune-1440)              | authored   | reviewed        | Different observers are not being told incompatible stories.                                                                                                                                                                                              |

| 1442  | [crash-safe](#rune-1442)              | authored   | reviewed        | Failure does not leave storage in nonsense.                                                                                                                                                                                                               |

| 1445  | [deterministic](#rune-1445)           | authored   | reviewed        | Given the same inputs, the same result should return. This is the antidote to drift, heisenbugs, and unreviewable behavior.                                                                                                                               |
| 1446  | [durable](#rune-1446)                 | authored   | reviewed        | Once acknowledged, the change really sticks.                                                                                                                                                                                                              |

| 1459  | [hermetic](#rune-1459)                | authored   | generated_draft | `hermetic` is a quality attributes and guarantees rune for turn quality language into invariants, budgets, and measurable envelopes; use it when the artifact needs the hermetic obligation named before work proceeds.                                   |
| 1460  | [high-availability](#rune-1460)       | authored   | generated_draft | `high-availability` is a quality attributes and guarantees rune for turn quality language into invariants, budgets, and measurable envelopes; use it when the artifact needs the high-availability obligation named before work proceeds.                 |
| 1461  | [idempotent](#rune-1461)              | authored   | reviewed        | The word that makes retries survivable: same request, same lasting effect. Shadow: duplicate charges, duplicate emails, and fear of recovery.                                                                                                             |
| 1462  | [immutable](#rune-1462)               | authored   | reviewed        | Once written, this thing does not change in place. Immutability buys clarity, replayability, and safer sharing at the cost of update convenience.                                                                                                         |
| 1463  | [incremental](#rune-1463)             | authored   | generated_draft | `incremental` is a quality attributes and guarantees rune for turn quality language into invariants, budgets, and measurable envelopes; use it when the artifact needs the incremental obligation named before work proceeds.                             |
| 1464  | [indexed](#rune-1464)                 | authored   | generated_draft | `indexed` is a quality attributes and guarantees rune for turn quality language into invariants, budgets, and measurable envelopes; use it when the artifact needs the indexed obligation named before work proceeds.                                     |
| 1465  | [isolated](#rune-1465)                | authored   | generated_draft | `isolated` is a quality attributes and guarantees rune for turn quality language into invariants, budgets, and measurable envelopes; use it when the artifact needs the isolated obligation named before work proceeds.                                   |
| 1466  | [lazy](#rune-1466)                    | authored   | generated_draft | `lazy` is a quality attributes and guarantees rune for turn quality language into invariants, budgets, and measurable envelopes; use it when the artifact needs the lazy obligation named before work proceeds.                                           |
| 1467  | [linearizable](#rune-1467)            | authored   | reviewed        | Every operation behaves as though it took effect at one real instant visible to all observers. Use it rarely, but mean it fully.                                                                                                                          |
| 1468  | [lock-free {Qual}](#rune-1468)        | authored   | generated_draft | `lock-free` in its Qual sense is a quality attributes and guarantees rune for turn quality language into invariants, budgets, and measurable envelopes; use it when the artifact needs the lock-free obligation named before work proceeds.               |

| 1471  | [maintainable](#rune-1471)            | authored   | reviewed        | Cheap enough to understand, modify, and keep healthy.                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| 1472  | [memory-safe {Qual}](#rune-1472)      | authored   | reviewed        | A promise that the system will not casually trespass across allocation boundaries. Shadow: leaks, corruption, and security bugs born from illegal touch.                                                                                                  |
| 1473  | [modular](#rune-1473)                 | authored   | generated_draft | `modular` is a quality attributes and guarantees rune for turn quality language into invariants, budgets, and measurable envelopes; use it when the artifact needs the modular obligation named before work proceeds.                                     |
| 1474  | [observable](#rune-1474)              | authored   | reviewed        | Structured so internal behavior can be inferred from external evidence.                                                                                                                                                                                   |

| 1481  | [pure](#rune-1481)                    | authored   | reviewed        | Same inputs, same output, no hidden state smuggled in from the side. Use it to shrink reasoning scope.                                                                                                                                                    |

| 1483  | [reentrant](#rune-1483)               | authored   | generated_draft | `reentrant` is a quality attributes and guarantees rune for turn quality language into invariants, budgets, and measurable envelopes; use it when the artifact needs the reentrant obligation named before work proceeds.                                 |
| 1484  | [reliable](#rune-1484)                | authored   | reviewed        | Keeps doing the promised thing under ordinary stress.                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| 1485  | [reproducible](#rune-1485)            | authored   | reviewed        | Can be rebuilt or rerun and yield the same result again.                                                                                                                                                                                                  |

| 1491  | [secure](#rune-1491)                  | authored   | generated_draft | `secure` is a quality attributes and guarantees rune for turn quality language into invariants, budgets, and measurable envelopes; use it when the artifact needs the secure obligation named before work proceeds.                                       |
| 1492  | [serializable {Qual}](#rune-1492)     | authored   | reviewed        | Concurrency forced to behave as though transactions happened in one clean order. Expensive when overused, indispensable when history must remain sane.                                                                                                    |

## Entries

### [1421] accessible {#rune-1421}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** generated_draft

**Force:** `accessible` is a quality attributes and guarantees rune for turn quality language into invariants, budgets, and measurable envelopes; use it when the artifact needs the accessible obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `accessible` at rune 1421 in the quality lane can let quality claims can become theater without checks; verify by requiring the caster to state the invariant, measurement, operating range, and failure threshold.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `accessible` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: `accessible` is a quality attributes and guarantees rune for turn quality language into invariants, budgets, and measurable envelopes; use it when the artifact needs the accessible obligation named before work proceeds.
- Ask the assistant to state the `accessible` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `accessible` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

### [1425] atomic {#rune-1425}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** reviewed

**Force:** No halfway state is allowed to leak across the line. Use it when partial success would be indistinguishable from corruption.

**Shadow:** when one layer promises indivisibility that another layer can still interleave.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `atomic` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: No halfway state is allowed to leak across the line. Use it when partial success would be indistinguishable from corruption.
- Ask the assistant to state the `atomic` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `atomic` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

**Pocket gloss:** No halfway state is allowed to leak.

### [1429] available {#rune-1429}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** reviewed

**Force:** Reachable and functioning when needed.

**Shadow:** misusing `available` at rune 1429 in the quality lane can let quality claims can become theater without checks; verify by requiring the caster to state the invariant, measurement, operating range, and failure threshold.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `available` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: Reachable and functioning when needed.
- Ask the assistant to state the `available` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `available` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

**Pocket gloss:** Reachable and functioning when needed.

### [1430] backward-compatible {#rune-1430}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** reviewed

**Force:** New versions do not casually break old clients or data.

**Shadow:** misusing `backward-compatible` at rune 1430 in the quality lane can let quality claims can become theater without checks; verify by requiring the caster to state the invariant, measurement, operating range, and failure threshold.

### [1445] deterministic {#rune-1445}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** reviewed

**Force:** Given the same inputs, the same result should return. This is the antidote to drift, heisenbugs, and unreviewable behavior.

**Shadow:** when deterministic output makes a wrong assumption reproducible instead of correct.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `deterministic` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: Given the same inputs, the same result should return. This is the antidote to drift, heisenbugs, and unreviewable behavior.
- Ask the assistant to state the `deterministic` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `deterministic` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

**Pocket gloss:** Same inputs, same result.

### [1446] durable {#rune-1446}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** reviewed

**Force:** Once acknowledged, the change really sticks.

**Shadow:** misusing `durable` at rune 1446 in the quality lane can let quality claims can become theater without checks; verify by requiring the caster to state the invariant, measurement, operating range, and failure threshold.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `durable` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: Once acknowledged, the change really sticks.
- Ask the assistant to state the `durable` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `durable` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

**Pocket gloss:** Once acknowledged, the change really sticks.

### [1461] idempotent {#rune-1461}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** reviewed

**Force:** The word that makes retries survivable: same request, same lasting effect.

**Shadow:** duplicate charges, duplicate emails, and fear of recovery.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `idempotent` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: The word that makes retries survivable: same request, same lasting effect.
- Ask the assistant to state the `idempotent` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `idempotent` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

**Pocket gloss:** Same request, same lasting effect.

### [1462] immutable {#rune-1462}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** reviewed

**Force:** Once written, this thing does not change in place. Immutability buys clarity, replayability, and safer sharing at the cost of update convenience.

**Shadow:** when immutability is shallow and hidden references still mutate underneath.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `immutable` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: Once written, this thing does not change in place. Immutability buys clarity, replayability, and safer sharing at the cost of update convenience.
- Ask the assistant to state the `immutable` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `immutable` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

**Pocket gloss:** Written once, then never changed in place.

### [1463] incremental {#rune-1463}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** generated_draft

**Force:** `incremental` is a quality attributes and guarantees rune for turn quality language into invariants, budgets, and measurable envelopes; use it when the artifact needs the incremental obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `incremental` at rune 1463 in the quality lane can let quality claims can become theater without checks; verify by requiring the caster to state the invariant, measurement, operating range, and failure threshold.

**Prompt uses:**

### [1467] linearizable {#rune-1467}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** reviewed

**Force:** Every operation behaves as though it took effect at one real instant visible to all observers. Use it rarely, but mean it fully.

**Shadow:** when a linearizable claim ignores the boundary, clock, or failure mode where order breaks.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `linearizable` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: Every operation behaves as though it took effect at one real instant visible to all observers. Use it rarely, but mean it fully.
- Ask the assistant to state the `linearizable` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `linearizable` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

**Pocket gloss:** Behaves as though each operation took effect at one visible instant.

**Force:** Cheap enough to understand, modify, and keep healthy.

- Ask the assistant to state the `maintainable` shadow before proposing changes.

**Pocket gloss:** Cheap enough to understand, modify, and keep healthy.

### [1472] memory-safe {Qual} {#rune-1472}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** reviewed

**Force:** A promise that the system will not casually trespass across allocation boundaries.

**Shadow:** leaks, corruption, and security bugs born from illegal touch.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `memory-safe` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: A promise that the system will not casually trespass across allocation boundaries.
- Ask the assistant to state the `memory-safe` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `memory-safe` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

**Pocket gloss:** Will not casually trespass across allocation boundaries.

- Name `observable` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: Structured so internal behavior can be inferred from external evidence.

**Pocket gloss:** Structured so internal behavior can be inferred from external evidence.

### [1481] pure {#rune-1481}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** reviewed

**Force:** Same inputs, same output, no hidden state smuggled in from the side. Use it to shrink reasoning scope.

**Shadow:** when a pure-looking function reads hidden state, time, randomness, or external effects.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `pure` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: Same inputs, same output, no hidden state smuggled in from the side. Use it to shrink reasoning scope.
- Ask the assistant to state the `pure` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `pure` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

**Pocket gloss:** Same inputs, same output, no hidden side effects.

### [1484] reliable {#rune-1484}

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** reviewed

**Shadow:** misusing `reliable` at rune 1484 in the quality lane can let quality claims can become theater without checks; verify by requiring the caster to state the invariant, measurement, operating range, and failure threshold.

**Prompt uses:**

**Pocket gloss:** Keeps doing the promised thing under ordinary stress.

### [1485] reproducible {#rune-1485}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** reviewed

**Force:** Can be rebuilt or rerun and yield the same result again.

**Shadow:** misusing `reproducible` at rune 1485 in the quality lane can let quality claims can become theater without checks; verify by requiring the caster to state the invariant, measurement, operating range, and failure threshold.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `reproducible` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: Can be rebuilt or rerun and yield the same result again.
- Ask the assistant to state the `reproducible` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `reproducible` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

**Pocket gloss:** Can be rebuilt or rerun and yield the same result again.

### [1492] serializable {Qual} {#rune-1492}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** reviewed

**Force:** Concurrency forced to behave as though transactions happened in one clean order. Expensive when overused, indispensable when history must remain sane.

**Shadow:** when serializable language hides weaker isolation, contention, or retry behavior.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `serializable` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: Concurrency forced to behave as though transactions happened in one clean order. Expensive when overused, indispensable when history must remain sane.
- Ask the assistant to state the `serializable` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `serializable` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

**Pocket gloss:** Concurrency behaves as though transactions happened in one clean order.
